git commit -F - <<'EOF'
phase 4.3: delegatable kernel scheduler + user-space SCHED server

Make the kernel run queue delegatable rather than replacing it (the
faithful MINIX 3 design). A per-proc `scheduler` endpoint defaults to
`NONE` (kernel-scheduled, the proven refill+rotate path); a SCHED-scheduled
proc that exhausts its quantum is dequeued, left RTS_NO_QUANTUM, and handed
to its user-space scheduler via a kernel-originated SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM
send, then re-admitted by a now-real SYS_SCHEDULE.

Kernel:
- `Proc::scheduler: Endpoint` (NONE default; populate_proc sets it).
- `sched::reschedule` branches on it; new `ipc::send::mini_sched_no_quantum_send`
  (a mini_pf_send clone) + `ipc::send_no_quantum` wrapper.
- Real `SYS_SCHEDULE` + new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` in `system/do_schedule.rs`, both
  target-taking and routed beside SYS_VMCTL. NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 = 15 (one-slice
  _PHASE3 alias); k_call_mask fill widened.
- `SCHED_RQ_BASE = 0xF00` request range (NO_QUANTUM/START/STOP/SET_NICE),
  const-asserted clear of VM/SEF/DS and below NOTIFY_MESSAGE; host tests.

SCHED server (servers/sched): SEF loop publishing to DS; round-robin quantum
refresh at band 8 (policy.rs, host-tested). Added to the MXBI boot image.
Priority aging (drop-a-band + balance_queues boost) deferred to 4.4's
SYS_SETALARM — without the boost it would starve the managed proc behind the
band-8 stubs. SCHEDULING_START/STOP/SET_NICE implemented for PM/RS to drive
from 4.5+; the kernel pre-delegates stub C as the live demo.

Verified in QEMU over 10 s: `[noq] proc=C -> scheduler=0x9` ->
`[ksys SYS_SCHEDULE] target=C prio=8 quantum=5 result=0` round-trip; stub C
sustains; eight `[as]` lines; A<->B + D's faults->VM intact; zero panic, every
line result=0. Host: kernel-shared/sched/server-rt/ds/vm tests green; check +
clippy -D warnings + fmt clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

input.command:
cd /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minixrs
gh pr create --base main --head feature/phase-4-3-sched \
  --title "phase 4.3: delegatable kernel scheduler + user-space SCHED server" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Slice 4.3 makes the kernel run queue **delegatable** rather than replacing it (the faithful MINIX 3 design), and stands up the user-space **SCHED** server. A per-proc `scheduler` endpoint defaults to `NONE` (kernel-scheduled — the proven refill+rotate path stays the fallback). A SCHED-scheduled proc that exhausts its quantum is dequeued, left `RTS_NO_QUANTUM`, and handed to its scheduler via a kernel-originated `SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM` send, then re-admitted by a now-real `SYS_SCHEDULE`. Single PR (the kernel-only half boots identically to 4.2, so it has no standalone milestone).

## Kernel

- `Proc::scheduler: Endpoint` (`NONE` default; `populate_proc` sets it, `Proc::EMPTY` zeroes it).
- `sched::reschedule` branches on it; new `ipc::send::mini_sched_no_quantum_send` (a `mini_pf_send` clone) + `ipc::send_no_quantum` wrapper that materializes the proc-table slice like `send_pagefault_to_vm`.
- Real `SYS_SCHEDULE` + new `SYS_SCHEDCTL` (`system/do_schedule.rs`), both target-taking and routed beside `SYS_VMCTL`. `NR_KERN_CALLS_PHASE4 = 15` (one-slice `_PHASE3` alias, dropped in 4.4); `init_boot_image` `k_call_mask` fill widened so SCHED may issue them.
- `SCHED_RQ_BASE = 0xF00` request range (`SCHEDULING_NO_QUANTUM`/`START`/`STOP`/`SET_NICE`), const-asserted clear of VM (`0xC00`) / SEF (`0xD00`) / DS (`0xE00`) and below `NOTIFY_MESSAGE`; SEF classifier returns `Application` for it unchanged.

## SCHED server (`servers/sched`)

- server-rt SEF loop publishing its endpoint to DS; handlers for all four `SCHEDULING_*`.
- Policy: round-robin quantum refresh at a fixed managed band (`USER_Q = 8`, the boot-server band) held in a static `[SchedProc; 16]` `UnsafeCell` table (`policy.rs`, pure helpers host-tested like `ds/registry.rs`).
- Added to the MXBI `servers` array in `build.rs` (proc_nr 9).

## Scope notes

- **Priority aging deferred to 4.4.** The decomposition called for "drop a band on full-quantum use"; that needs MINIX's compensating periodic `balance_queues` boost, which needs `SYS_SETALARM` (slice 4.4). Without it, sinking the managed proc's band would starve it behind the kernel-scheduled band-8 stubs, violating the "no starvation" check — so 4.3 ships a same-band refresh. Documented in `policy.rs`.
- `SCHEDULING_START`/`STOP`/`SET_NICE` + `SYS_SCHEDCTL` are implemented + host-tested for PM/RS to drive from 4.5/4.6; the live exercise pre-delegates stub C (`userland.rs` sets its `scheduler = boot_endpoint(SCHED_PROC_NR)`).

## Verification

**QEMU (10 s):** the round-trip is explicit —
```
C[noq 1] proc=C nr=13 -> scheduler=0x9
[ksys SYS_SCHEDULE] target=C nr=13 prio=8 quantum=5 result=0
```
Eight `[as]` lines (vm/ds/vfs/sched asid 1–4, stubs A–D asid 5–8); SCHED boots through SEF (`[ipc 10/11] caller=9` GET_WHOAMI + DS publish); stub C sustains (`[ksys] caller=13` to sample 250,800); A↔B ping-pong + D's three `[pf]` (brk×2 + mmap) resolved by VM all intact; every line `result=0`; zero panic / `el0_sync_unexpected`. (A/B/D keep `scheduler == NONE`, so they take the byte-identical pre-4.2 reschedule path.)

**Host:** `cargo test -p minixrs-kernel-shared -p minixrs-sched -p minixrs-server-rt -p minixrs-ds -p minixrs-vm` green (kernel-shared +3 SCHED callnr tests, sched 6 policy tests). `cargo check --workspace`, `clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `fmt --check`, and `cargo kernel-aarch64` all clean.

## Docs

`docs/plan.md` reconciled (4.2 → `✓ shipped PR #24, merged 2026-06-21`; 4.3 → `◀ ready`; 4.4 → `◀ next`); CLAUDE.md gains the delegatable-scheduler convention.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

content.text:
Done. PR #25 is open: **https://github.com/minixrs/minixrs/pull/25**

## Summary

Slice 4.3 is committed (GPG-signed), pushed, and the PR is open.

- **Commit** `6910691` — `phase 4.3: delegatable kernel scheduler + user-space SCHED server` (18 files, +1099/−61), signed with a Good GPG signature.
- **Branch** `feature/phase-4-3-sched` → pushed to origin.
- **PR #25** → `main`, with full summary, scope notes, and verification evidence.

CI's seven gates (fmt, clippy, audit, deny, geiger, miri, coverage) + the Sonar scan will now run on the PR. Locally everything was already green: `cargo check --workspace`, `clippy -D warnings`, `fmt --check`, `cargo kernel-aarch64`, the QEMU round-trip trace, and host tests across kernel-shared/sched/server-rt/ds/vm.

One thing worth flagging for your review on the PR: the **same-band refresh vs drop-a-band** decision (documented in `policy.rs` and the PR body) — I deferred priority aging to 4.4 because it needs `SYS_SETALARM` for the compensating `balance_queues` boost, without which a band-drop would starve stub C behind the kernel-scheduled band-8 stubs. If you'd rather see band movement demonstrated some other way in 4.3, that's the spot to push back.
